question_title: Which of these market indexes will perform the best during April 2026?
summary: The Nikkei 225 leads April 2026 with +9.55% MTD (from April 1 open), holding a 5.37 percentage point lead over #2 FTSE 100 (+4.18%). This large lead makes Nikkei the strong favorite to finish as best performer. However, significant geopolitical risk clouds the outlook: US-Iran peace talks collapsed on April 11-12 without agreement, Trump is threatening a Hormuz blockade, and the fragile ceasefire expires ~April 22 within the forecast window. Japan's heavy oil import dependency through Hormuz makes the Nikkei uniquely vulnerable, while FTSE 100's energy stock weighting (BP, Shell) positions it to outperform if oil spikes. Monte Carlo simulations across four geopolitical scenarios (deal 15%, fragile hold 25%, collapse 40%, escalation 20%) yield Nikkei ~48%, FTSE ~18%, S&P ~13%. Correlated selloffs tend to preserve existing rankings, so only differential shocks (specifically anti-Nikkei oil disruption) would flip the winner.

factors.description: Nikkei 225 has a commanding 9.55% April MTD gain vs 4.18% for #2 FTSE - a 5.37pp lead with 14 trading days remaining
Nikkei has highest daily volatility (2.37%, annualized 37.6%), making lead somewhat vulnerable but also capable of extending
US-Iran peace talks collapsed April 11-12; Trump threatening Hormuz blockade. Ceasefire expires ~April 22. Japan is most oil-import-dependent among these economies
FTSE 100 has significant energy company weighting (BP, Shell) that benefit from oil price spikes - uniquely positioned to outperform in escalation scenarios
S&P 500 currently #3 at +3.97% with lowest volatility among the six, providing defensive characteristics in risk-off environment
Even in ceasefire collapse scenario, Nikkei's expected performance (+4.0% after oil-shock drift) still leads FTSE (+3.5%). Only severe escalation flips the ranking
Correlated global selloffs preserve existing rankings - only differential/idiosyncratic shocks change the winner. Most market moves are correlated

reasoning:
**Summary of forecast:**

| Index | Probability |
|-------|------------|
| **Nikkei 225** | **48%** |
| FTSE 100 | 18% |
| S&P 500 | 13% |
| Hang Seng | 7% |
| CAC 40 | 7% |
| DAX 40 | 7% |

**Key reasoning:**
- Nikkei 225 has a dominant **+9.55%** April MTD gain, leading FTSE 100 by 5.37 percentage points — a substantial cushion
- The base Monte Carlo gives Nikkei ~64% win probability, but I adjust downward to 48% due to the **failed US-Iran peace talks** (April 11-12) and Trump's Hormuz blockade threat
- Japan's oil dependency through Hormuz makes Nikkei uniquely vulnerable to escalation
- FTSE 100 is the main beneficiary in conflict scenarios (energy stock weighting), elevated to 18%
- Correlated selloffs would preserve rankings; only differential shocks flip the winner

[(Metaculus) Which of these market indexes will perform the best during April 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43105)
resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as the market index which increases the most or decreases the least percent during April 2026.
fine_print:
Specifically, this question will resolve based upon the difference between the opening price on April 1, 2026, and the closing price on April 30, 2026.

The primary source for this question will be [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/world-indices/). If Yahoo Finance is unavailable or found to be inaccurate, another source may be used.

***
This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43105) which opened on 2026-04-08 17:00:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print of the linked original question. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
